message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Adobe Grill (La Quinta)
Cuisine/type signal: Regional Mexican / tequila bar
Why it surfaced in discovery: Resort-anchor Mexican restaurant at La Quinta Resort & Club with 100+ tequila selection and award-winning tamales; signature Oaxacan-motif room with double-sided fireplace
Discovery sources: https://www.laquintaresort.com/dining/adobe-grill/ | https://www.hilton.com/en/hotels/psplqqq-la-quinta-resort-and-club/dining/adobe-grill/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

input.prompt: Extract: restaurant description, cuisine, hours, menu structure, location, schema.org markup signals, awards or recognitions mentioned, any tequila/tamales info, history.

input.query: Adobe Grill La Quinta Resort reviews Yelp TripAdvisor

content.content:
# Adobe Grill at La Quinta Resort & Club

## Restaurant Description
Adobe Grill offers "an authentic Mexican dining experience with our traditional Oaxacan motif and double-sided fireplace." The venue emphasizes craft cocktails, featuring over 100 tequila options crafted by skilled bartenders.

## Cuisine
Mexican cuisine with Oaxacan influences, featuring traditional preparations alongside contemporary dishes.

## Hours of Operation
- **Dinner:** 5pm–10pm daily
- **Lunch:** Friday–Saturday, 10am–3pm
- **Happy Hour:** 3pm–5pm (bar only)
- **Live Entertainment:** Thursday and Sunday, 6:30pm–9pm

## Location
49-499 Eisenhower Drive, La Quinta, California, 92253

## Menu Structure
The restaurant organizes offerings into distinct categories:
- Lunch and Dinner salads
- Appetizers (antojitos, crudos, ceviches)
- Signature specialties (enchiladas, moles, carne asada)
- Fajitas and tacos
- Extensive tequila selection (Blanco, Reposado, Añejo, Extra Añejo, Mezcal varieties)
- Margarita varieties (including Cadillac, El Perfecto, Pepino Diablo)
- Wine, beer, and cocktails
- Desserts

## Notable Details
The beverage program features margarita customization and an expansive tequila collection representing multiple categories and flavor profiles, reflecting the restaurant's focus on Mexican spirits expertise.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Adobe Grill at La Quinta Resort reviews on Yelp and TripAdvisor:

## Overview
Adobe Grill features mariachi music and colorful surroundings for refined, authentic regional Mexican cuisine, with signature dishes including Caldo de Mariscos, Ceviche de Mariscos Marinera, and tableside guacamole.

## Review Highlights

**Positive Aspects:**
- The restaurant is known throughout the Valley for the best margaritas in town, served in oversized, hand-blown glassware from Guadalajara
- The ambience is festive and picturesque with hacienda-style décor, twinkly lights, live mariachi/jazz and a lively outdoor patio, with guests calling it a 'magical' or 'must-do' experience in La Quinta
- Some reviews praise the excellent service and note that everything they ate was delicious

**Criticisms:**
- Some reviewers noted the large restaurant churns customers quickly, with servers rushing entrees out while diners were still eating appetizers, and complaints about tough shrimp and boring sauce
- One reviewer called it the most expensive and worst meal they've had in a Mexican restaurant, criticizing the Carne Asada as "inedible shoe leather"
- Indoor seating can be loud and acoustically bright, with live music and large parties making conversation difficult on busy nights

## Overall Rating
On TripAdvisor, Adobe Grill is ranked #9 of 142 restaurants in La Quinta with 646 reviews.

content.content:
# Adobe Grill - La Quinta Summary

**Total Reviews:** 653

**Rating:** 4.0 out of 5 bubbles

**Restaurant Ranking:** #7 of 127 restaurants in La Quinta

**Recent Review Themes (2025-2026):**
- Positive: Outstanding bar service, excellent margaritas, beautiful outdoor ambiance with mountain views
- Mixed: Food quality inconsistencies; some praised enchiladas and fresh dishes, while others found them undercooked or bland
- Service variability noted across recent visits

**Awards & Recognition:**
- Travelers' Choice award recipient (ranked within top 10% on Tripadvisor)
- Known throughout the valley for signature Cadillac margaritas served in hand-blown glassware from Guadalajara

**Notable Features:** Located on upper level of La Quinta Resort & Club plaza; offers live mariachi music (Saturday-Thursday) and live jazz trio (Friday nights); features refined regional Mexican cuisine with tableside guacamole preparation.

Links: [{"title":"Adobe Grill Mexican Restaurant | La Quinta Resort & Club","url":"https://www.laquintaresort.com/dining/adobe-grill/"},{"title":"Adobe Grill | La Quinta Resort & Club","url":"https://map.laquintaresort.com/dining/adobe-grill"},{"title":"Adobe Grill | Villas at La Quinta Resort","url":"http://www.villasatlaquintaresort.com/dining/adobe-grill.aspx"},{"title":"ADOBE GRILL - Updated June 2026 - 544 Photos & 612 Reviews - 49-499 Eisenhower Dr, La Quinta, California - Mexican - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/adobe-grill-la-quinta"},{"title":"Discover Authentic Oaxacan Flavors at La Quinta’s Adobe Grill | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/discover-authentic-oaxacan-flavors-at-la-quintas-adobe-grill/"},{"title":"Adobe Grill at La Quinta Resort & Club | La Quinta, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/adobe-grill-at-la-quinta-resort-&-club/5879/"},{"title":"Adobe Grill - La Quinta Mexican Restaurants and Bars","url":"https://directory.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/adobe-grill/"},{"title":"Dining Around The Desert: Adobe Grill At La Quinta Resort & Club","url":"https://yourresorthome.com/palm-springs-real-estate-blog/dining-around-the-desert-adobe-grill-at-la-quinta-resort-clu.html"},{"title":"Adobe Grill - Dog-Friendly Restaurants in La Quinta","url":"https://dogtrekker.com/directories/adobe-grill/"},{"title":"Adobe Grill Restaurant - La Quinta, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/adobe-grill-la-quinta-1"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Adobe Grill at La Quinta:

Adobe Grill is famed for its award-winning tamales and signature margaritas, and it's located at La Quinta Resort & Club. The restaurant offers authentic Mexican dining with a traditional Oaxacan motif and double-sided fireplace.

Regarding tequila, their talented bartenders can create a bespoke margarita from more than 100 tequilas. The restaurant features a 10-foot-high Aztec pyramid displaying various tequilas.

As for the tamales, the award-winning pork tamales can be ordered à la carte or with a combo platter. The tamales are consistently highlighted as a specialty of the restaurant across multiple sources.

The search results confirm the restaurant's recognition for both its award-winning tamales and its extensive tequila selection, though they don't provide specific details about particular awards or "best of" recognitions beyond the general references to award-winning status.

Based on the search results for the best Mexican restaurants in the Coachella Valley and La Quinta area (updated for 2026), here are some top recommendations:

## Top Mexican Restaurants in La Quinta & Coachella Valley:

**Casa Mendoza** and **Los Arcos Mexican Restaurant** are among the best Mexican options near La Quinta.

**Adobe Grill** is another excellent choice: Adobe Grill in La Quinta is known for its "fantastic Mexican food in the Coachella Valley" and delights diners with authentic dishes like Chicken Mole and Ceviche de Mariscos, complemented by "fabulous guacamole". The lively atmosphere is enhanced by nightly live music, creating an unforgettable dining experience.

**La Hacienda Mexican Restaurant** also appears frequently in recommendations as a top choice with "great food, good customer service and good prices".

**La Cazuela Restaurant** in Coachella is worth visiting: La Cazuela is one of the best for Mexican food with an extensive menu containing all the old favorites including tacos with various fillings, enchiladas, chile verde, and mariscos (seafood).

For seafood-focused Mexican cuisine, Mariscos El Capitan in Coachella is a family-owned spot known for its authentic mariscos (Mexican seafood), from fresh ceviches and shrimp cocktails to whole fish and house specialties, with live Banda music transforming dinner into a full-on night out.

Based on the search results, here are the best fine dining restaurants at La Quinta resort for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Options at La Quinta Resort

**Morgan's in the Desert**

Morgan's in the Desert is the undisputed anchor of fine dining in La Quinta at the La Quinta Resort & Club. It has been operating since 1926, and it offers a menu showcasing seasonal ingredients and innovative culinary techniques under renowned chef Jimmy Schmidt. The setting features warm lighting, lush courtyard surroundings, meticulous plating, and a considered wine list, with OpenTable recognition for best service.

**Adobe Grill**

Adobe Grill offers an authentic Mexican dining experience with a traditional Oaxacan motif, double-sided fireplace, and talented bartenders who create bespoke margaritas from more than 100 tequilas.

**Additional Notable Dining at the Resort**

Morgan's showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley, sourcing the region's top products and crafting them with traditional cooking methods. There is also a modern American bistro that showcases innovative approaches to familiar favorites using locally sourced, seasonal ingredients.

For reservations, reservations at Morgan's in the Desert and other top establishments should be made well in advance during high season (November through April) – ideally a week or more out, longer for weekend evenings.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Adobe Grill at La Quinta:

**Chef:** Chef Marco Aguilar leads the kitchen at Adobe Grill.

**Cuisine & Reputation:** Adobe Grill is famed for its award-winning tamales and signature margaritas, featuring authentic regional Mexican cuisine with salsas and tortillas made fresh daily. The restaurant serves authentic Oaxacan homestyle dishes with a seafood-forward menu and a 100-variety tequila bar.

**Year Opened:** Unfortunately, the search results do not contain specific information about when Adobe Grill opened. The results mention that La Quinta Resort has been welcoming legendary guests since 1926, but no specific opening year for the Adobe Grill restaurant itself was found in these sources.

If you need more detailed historical information about when Adobe Grill specifically opened, you may want to contact La Quinta Resort directly.

Web search results for query: "regional Mexican restaurant Palm Springs date night romantic"

Links: [{"title":"Where to Go for Date Night in the Coachella Valley | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/where-to-go-for-date-night-in-the-coachella-valley/"},{"title":"Tac/Quila - Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://tacquila.com/"},{"title":"Best Mexican Food in Palm Springs: Tacos, Traditions & Modern Kitchens","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/best-mexican-food-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Home | Las Casuelas Terraza","url":"https://www.lascasuelas.com/"},{"title":"The 50 best Mexican foods and restaurants in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://wanderlog.com/list/geoCategory/776903/best-mexican-foods-and-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs"},{"title":"The 28 best romantic restaurants in Palm Springs","url":"https://wanderlog.com/list/geoCategory/79311/best-romantic-restaurants-in-palm-springs"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Mexican Restaurants in Palm Springs (Updated 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32847-c29-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Clandestino Restaurant - Palm Springs, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/clandestino-palm-springs"}]

Based on the search results, here are some excellent regional Mexican restaurants in Palm Springs perfect for a romantic date night:

## Top Recommendations

**Tac/Quila**
Tac Quila is Michelin rated and listed in the guide for the past four or five years with elevated Mexican cuisine and cocktails. It offers an intimate dining experience with creative dishes and thoughtfully crafted cocktails.

**Clandestino**
Clandestino is a popular pick for date night with guests praising the moody, stylish patio with mountain views, craft cocktails (margarita flights are a favorite), and cozy heaters/blankets for cool nights. The restaurant serves elevated and fresh Mexican food located right next to the Marilyn Monroe statue in downtown Palm Springs.

**Las Casuelas Terraza**
Since 1958, they have been serving four generations of Delgado family recipes to visitors and locals in Palm Springs. The restaurant offers one of three patio dining experiences, from the lively Palapa patio to the romantic Terraza patio with its sparkling fountain and flower display or the intimate Cantina sidewalk patio.

**Maleza**
Located in Smoke Tree Village in South Palm Springs, Maleza offers a refined, contemporary approach to Mexican cooking, often incorporating seasonal produce and coastal influence, with plates that balance tradition with modern presentation.

Palm Springs has one of Southern California's most vibrant Mexican food scenes, shaped by generations of local families, desert agriculture, cross-border influences, and a modern wave of chefs redefining regional cuisine.

# Structured Data Analysis: Adobe Grill Page

## Schema.org & Structured Data Assessment

**Status: No detectable structured data found**

The webpage lacks:
- ✗ JSON-LD markup
- ✗ Schema.org restaurant schema
- ✗ Opening hours/hours of operation markup
- ✗ Microdata attributes
- ✗ Organized Event schema
- ✗ Menu Item schema

## Available Information (Unstructured)

The page provides operational details in plain HTML without machine-readable markup:

**Hours Listed:**
- "**Dinner:** 5pm - 10pm"
- "**Lunch:** Fri-Sat 10am - 3pm"
- Happy Hour: 3pm - 5pm (bar only)
- Live Entertainment: Thursdays & Sundays 6:30pm - 9pm

**Contact Information:**
- Address: 49-499 Eisenhower Drive, La Quinta, California 92253
- Phone: 760.564.4111

## Recommendations

For improved SEO and discoverability, implementing JSON-LD restaurant schema would enable search engines to properly index hours, menu items, location data, and reservations capabilities.

content.text: I have enough information to complete the assessment. Let me compile the structured output.

input.name: Adobe Grill

reputation.sentiment: mostly positive with mixed notes on consistency
reputation.recency_signal: Recent (2025-2026) reviews praise margaritas, bar service, outdoor mountain-view ambiance, and live mariachi/jazz; criticisms cite food quality inconsistency (carne asada, shrimp), rushed service on busy nights, and high resort pricing relative to value.
reputation.longevity: [UNVERIFIED] La Quinta Resort dates to 1926; Adobe Grill's specific opening year not surfaced in public sources. Resort currently flagged as Waldorf Astoria / Hilton portfolio (ownership stability tied to parent flag).
credentials: TripAdvisor #7-9 of ~127-142 restaurants in La Quinta
Frequently cited in Palm Springs Life and Visit Greater Palm Springs dining roundups
Widely referenced 'award-winning tamales' claim [UNVERIFIED — no specific award body or year located]

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably on 'best Mexican restaurant La Quinta', 'fine dining La Quinta resort', and 'La Quinta Resort dining' queries. Less consistent on broader 'best Mexican Coachella Valley' or 'regional Mexican Palm Springs' queries, where Tac/Quila (Michelin), Las Casuelas Terraza, Clandestino, and Maleza dominate. Does not surface for 'date night Palm Springs' tier queries despite romantic-room positioning.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When surfaced, descriptions are generally accurate: regional Mexican, Oaxacan motif, 100+ tequila program, signature margaritas, live mariachi. Chef attribution (Marco Aguilar) appears in some search snippets but not confirmed on the resort site.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Weak. Resort page (laquintaresort.com/dining/adobe-grill) contains no JSON-LD, no Restaurant schema, no machine-readable hours or menu. Hours and address are present only as plain HTML. Listing depends entirely on third-party aggregators (OpenTable, Yelp, TripAdvisor, VisitGreaterPalmSprings) for agent-readable structure.

competitors: El Jefe at Saguaro (Palm Springs) and other resort-Mexican concepts
relationships.corridor_role: anchor (within La Quinta Resort corridor); supporting in broader Coachella Valley Mexican fine-dining bracket where Palm Springs concepts lead

convertibility.ownership_signal: resort-owned (La Quinta Resort & Club, Waldorf Astoria / Hilton portfolio)
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Marketing is bundled into the parent resort and Hilton brand stack — dedicated subpages on laquintaresort.com and hilton.com, OpenTable presence, syndicated DMO listings (VisitGreaterPalmSprings, playinlaquinta.com). No independent paid social, agency footprint, or standalone restaurant domain.
convertibility.reasoning: Adobe Grill is a resort-operated F&B outlet inside the Waldorf Astoria-flagged La Quinta Resort & Club. There is no reachable independent owner-operator; decisions about menu, marketing, web presence, and schema sit with resort F&B leadership and Hilton corporate. There is no separate domain, no independent paid marketing footprint, and no signal of an owner with budget authority to commission an agent-visibility project. Classify as anchor-reference — useful in the corridor map for competitive context (it's a legitimate anchor in the La Quinta resort dining bracket) but not a convertible client.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Specific opening year of Adobe Grill — only the resort's 1926 founding is documented, not the restaurant concept's launch date.
[UNVERIFIED] 'Award-winning tamales' is widely repeated across listings and the resort's own copy but no specific awarding body, publication, or year was located.
[UNVERIFIED] Chef Marco Aguilar attribution surfaced in a search snippet but is not confirmed on the resort's own dining page.
[UNVERIFIED] Yelp star rating — Yelp page returned 403 to direct fetch; review count band (~612) is from search index snippet, not direct verification.
Palm Springs Life feature URL returned 403 on direct fetch — content referenced via search index only.
